prompt: We are a state government IT team responsible for hardening a new Windows Server 2022 deployment. Walk us through what the ISM Chapter 13 (System Hardening) requires and what evidence we need to collect to demonstrate compliance during an assessment.
assertions: References ISM Chapter 13 (System Hardening) as the relevant guideline
Covers OS hardening requirements: disable unnecessary services, remove/rename default accounts, apply ASD-approved hardening guide
Mentions application allow-listing as a key ISM/Essential Eight control
States least privilege principle: no standing administrative access; use of Privileged Access Management (PAM)
Lists evidence types required: hardening checklists, configuration screenshots or CIS benchmark scan results, PAM solution logs, software allow-list policy
